name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent dissemination of false information, conspiracy theories, and hateful rhetoric. He has repeatedly promoted debunked claims, including those related to COVID-19, election fraud, and social issues, often targeting marginalized groups such as LGBTQ individuals, Muslims, and people of color. Kirk has also expressed support for Christian nationalism, advocated for the suppression of minority rights, and called for violence against certain groups. His actions and statements demonstrate a blatant disregard for facts, truth, and human dignity, and have contributed to the spread of misinformation and the erosion of civil discourse. Kirk's behavior is characterized by a willingness to use inflammatory language, promote divisive ideologies, and engage in hate speech, making him a prominent figure in the spread of extremist views.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk promoted debunked claims about George Floyd, such as that he was 'illegally counterfeiting currency' and had once 'put a gun to a pregnant woman's stomach'.
Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet that spread false information about human trafficking arrests after a fact-checker pointed out the false number originated on the conspiracy site 8chan.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'.
Charlie Kirk promoted false allegations of electoral fraud in the 2020 election.
Charlie Kirk was considered a 'big name' social influencer in Rudy Giuliani's communications plan to overturn the 2020 election.

Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media, including sharply criticizing Democrats' criticism of Trump's withdrawal of WHO funding and calling COVID-19 the 'China virus'.

Charlie Kirk alleged that the WHO covered up information about the COVID-19 pandemic and was briefly banned from Twitter after falsely claiming that hydroxychloroquine had proved to be '100% effective in treating the virus'.
Charlie Kirk falsely stated that during the 2009 swine flu pandemic it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency.

Charlie Kirk described the public health measure of social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity and made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients.
Charlie Kirk refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'.

Charlie Kirk released his book The MAGA Doctrine, a manifesto for the Make America Great Again movement, in which he wrote that the Republican Party is 'in some sense no longer a conservative party, no longer the party of Reagan, but instead a Trump-remade populist party'.

Charlie Kirk said at a Council for National Policy meeting that Democrats shutting down college campuses would remove opportunities for ballot harvesting and voter fraud, and that it would be beneficial to keep campuses closed.
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix, promoting false and disproven claims of fraud in the election.
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 "buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for this president".
Charlie Kirk's organizations received $1.25 million from Publix heiress Julie Fancelli to fund buses to the January 6 event.
Charlie Kirk paid $60,000 for Kimberly Guilfoyle to speak at the rally.
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked her husband with a hammer.
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination while appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack.
Charlie Kirk promoted COVID-19 misinformation.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A Brookings Institution study found Charlie Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters.
Charlie Kirk advocated for Christian nationalism and criticized the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and Martin Luther King Jr.
Charlie Kirk promoted the white genocide conspiracy theory.
Trump appointed Charlie Kirk to the U.S. Air Force Academy Board of Visitors.
Charlie Kirk expressed concerns that the Trump administration's crackdowns on campuses threatened free speech and were a weaponization of antisemitism.
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was a part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'.
Charlie Kirk was assassinated by a rooftop sniper while speaking at a TPUSA debate event at Utah Valley University.
Charlie Kirk told a college audience that the concept of white privilege is a myth and a 'racist idea'
Charlie Kirk presented what the Southern Poverty Law Center described as 'a symphony of racist dog-whistles ... pandering to the extreme right' at the 2018 Western Conservative Summit.
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC

Charlie Kirk stated that Democratic immigration policies were aimed at 'diminishing and decreasing white demographics in America' and called for Texas to 'deputize a citizen force and put them on the border' to protect 'white demographics in America'

Charlie Kirk wrote in a Fox News article that 'directly confronting the left, and promising to fight their illiberal ideology with state power when necessary, is the key to winning everyday Americans'
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines.
Charlie Kirk called mandatory requirements for students to take the COVID-19 vaccine 'medical apartheid' on the Fox News show hosted by Tucker Carlson.
Charlie Kirk told young women to go to college for the purpose of finding husbands and 'embrace their roles as mothers and homemakers'

Charlie Kirk said that Democrats want Americans to live in a society with 'no cultural identity, where you live in sexual anarchy, where private property is a thing of the past, and the ruling class controls everything'
Charlie Kirk wrote an op-ed stating that 'the facts are that there are only two genders; that transgenderism and gender 'fluidity' are lies that hurt people and abuse kids'
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay was an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction.
Charlie Kirk routinely made anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'
Charlie Kirk said that the separation of church and state in the United States is a 'fabrication'.

Charlie Kirk said that 'all men are created equal in the eyes of God, all men and women, but not all cultures are created equal. To say that, you get attacked in every direction, but excuse me when I say that Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced. It's an outgrowth of the Bible.'
Charlie Kirk said that 'prowling Blacks go around for fun to go target white people' in urban America.
Charlie Kirk stated that the Civil Rights Act of 1964 was a 'huge mistake' and had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'.
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'.
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'
Charlie Kirk stated that the transgender issue is 'so against our senses, so against the natural law and, dare I say, a throbbing middle finger to God'

Charlie Kirk said, 'I think it's worth it, I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights.'

Charlie Kirk reacted to Representative Sheila Jackson Lee's statement on affirmative action by saying 'If we would have said three weeks ago [...] that Joy Reid and Michelle Obama and Sheila Jackson Lee and Ketanji Brown Jackson were affirmative-action picks, we would have been called racist. But now they're comin' out and they're saying it for us!'
Charlie Kirk said 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'.
Charlie Kirk said 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white.'
Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality'.

Charlie Kirk said, 'One of the reasons we're living through a constitutional crisis is that we no longer have a Christian nation, but we have a Christian form of government, and they're incompatible. You cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population.'
Charlie Kirk said, 'This is a Christian state. I'd like to see it stay that way.'
Charlie Kirk called for a nationwide ban of gender-affirming care for transgender people and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for doctors who perform such care

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for celebrating Pride Month, quoting the Bible verse 'love thy neighbor' and arguing that she was being selective, while also referencing Leviticus 18 which states 'thou shall lay with another man shall be stoned to death'
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse.
Charlie Kirk advanced pro-Russian talking points about the Russo-Ukrainian War
Charlie Kirk argued that 'It's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize our civilization'
Charlie Kirk blamed the Chinese government and drug cartels for the opioid crisis in the United States
Charlie Kirk called for Mehdi Hasan to be deported and deplatformed over his views on the COVID-19 pandemic
Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips
Charlie Kirk denied that Israel is starving Palestinians
Charlie Kirk opposed U.S. involvement in the Iran–Israel War
Charlie Kirk opposed the U.S. sending arms to Ukraine or providing financial support to the country
Charlie Kirk said of Palestine, 'I don't think the place exists'
Charlie Kirk said immigration to the United States should be completely stopped
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation
Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the October 7 attacks on Israel in 2023

Charlie Kirk said on The Charlie Kirk Show that 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies ... This is a beast created by secular Jews, and now it's coming for Jews', and also suggested that these Jews control 'not just the colleges; it's the nonprofits, it's the movies, it's Hollywood, it's all of it.'
Charlie Kirk blamed DEI programs for national aviation issues, saying, 'If I see a Black pilot, I'm going to be like, 'Boy, I hope he's qualified.'
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' and said she was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped

Charlie Kirk said 'America does not need more visas for people from India', arguing that the American workforce has become dominated by Indian-American immigrants, effectively decreasing job opportunities for Americans

Charlie Kirk said that a 'myth' had been created around Martin Luther King Jr. which had 'grown totally out of control' and that King was currently 'the most honored, worshiped, even deified person of the 20th century' despite 'most people' supposedly disliking him during his life
Charlie Kirk commented on how race is less important to culture than religion is, stating that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians

Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism, stating: 'When you have multiple gods, you get different moralities. And the West has largely embraced the idea that there is a standard of conduct, or a best way to live.'
Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel
Charlie Kirk said the bill to expand anti-BDS laws would 'only create more antisemitism, and play into growing narratives that Israel is running the U.S. government'
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the July 2025 Central Texas floods on DEI
Charlie Kirk claimed that the philosophical foundation of anti-whiteness has been largely financed by Jewish donors
Charlie Kirk warned his followers against hatred of Jews, calling it evil and demonic
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine

Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that 'there is a relentless assault against Black people on behalf of white people', and said 'White individuals are actually more likely to be attacked, especially even per capita, by Black individuals in this country.'
Charlie Kirk posted that 24 years ago a group of Muslims killed 2,753 people on 9/11, and now a Muslim Socialist is on pace to run New York City, which was described as a gross and Islamophobic post
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America
Charlie Kirk opposed the Paris Agreement on climate change.
Charlie Kirk admitted that Turning Point USA had accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry and spoke out against targeting fossil fuels.
Charlie Kirk said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Charlie Kirk claimed in a video that there is no factual data to back up global warming and that scientists do not know the cause, which was later rated as inaccurate by Science Feedback.
Charlie Kirk referred to George Floyd as a 'scumbag'
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism and likening it to pseudo-paganism.
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion.
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a “stand down” order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks.
Charlie Kirk used his platforms to decry racial equity programs, float conspiracy theories, and test out divisive messaging that Mr. Trump has later adopted.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating 'very few Americans want war with you' and that 'the people obsessed with fighting you forever' were a minority 'on their way out of power'
Charlie Kirk promoted Christian Nationalism and far-right politically conservative politics through his organization, Turning Point USA.
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster'
Charlie Kirk stated that if the US took Taiwan, it would probably start a nuclear war and that US leaders have mishandled China.
Charlie Kirk accused Rep. Jasmine Crockett of being part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk was shot dead at a Utah university rally, with the shooter still at large, in what was described as a 'political assassination'.
Charlie Kirk's rhetoric was long cast as racist, xenophobic and extreme by groups that study hate speech, including the Southern Poverty Law Center.
President Trump called for prayers for Charlie Kirk on Truth Social after the shooting.
The Trump administration instructed staff to identify and discipline service members who mocked or expressed approval of Kirk's murder.
Charlie Kirk made statements about 'prowling blacks' and had views on gun control, according to his own words.
A public vigil was held for Charlie Kirk at the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, attended by Trump administration officials and Republican lawmakers.
Right-wing activists and members of the Trump administration demanded that people allegedly celebrating Kirk's death be silenced and fired, leading to comparisons with McCarthyism and cancel culture.

Charlie Kirk's accused assassin was charged with aggravated murder, felony discharge of a firearm causing serious bodily injury, two counts of obstruction of justice, two counts of witness tampering, and commission of a violent offense in the presence of a child.

A memorial service for Charlie Kirk was held at State Farm Stadium in Glendale, Arizona, attended by prominent figures including President Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
